data.title: Is GE Aerospace (GE) a Good Stock to Buy before Earnings?
data.description:
Aircraft company GE Aerospace (GE) is set to report its fourth-quarter earnings results on January 22 before the market opens. Wall Street is expecting solid results, as analysts are forecasting earnings per share of around $1.44. This is up from $1.32 in the same quarter last year. Meanwhile, revenue is projected to come in at around $11.2 billion, thanks to the strong demand for commercial engines and related services, the firm's largest segment, as shown in the image below.

data.title: How SOFI Technologies Appeared on Traders' Radars Ahead of Earnings
data.description:
Truth be told, the market has spent years trying to put SoFi Technologies (SOFI) into a tidy little box. It was first seen as a student lender, then as a bank, and then as a tech platform. But as we approach their fourth-quarter earnings release at the tail end of January, it's becoming clear that the "Amazon of FinTech" label isn't just marketing fluff anymore.

data.title: V or MA: Which Is the Better Stock Ahead of Earnings?
data.description:
With the December quarter earnings season at the forefront, it's time to gauge which credit card giant, Visa (V) or Mastercard (MA), is the better stock ahead of results. Visa will release its first quarter fiscal 2026 results on January 22, followed by Mastercard's fourth quarter fiscal 2025 results on January 29. Based on TipRanks' analyst consensus rating, both Visa and Mastercard are rated "Strong Buy," with MA shares offering slightly higher upside potential over the next twelve months.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Analysts expect both companies to report healthy growth in earnings and sales, driven by increasing consumer spending. Over the past year, V stock has gained 2.7% and MA shares have risen 2.8%. Both companies tend to move in sync, affected largely by the same macroeconomic and geopolitical factors. Both stocks also pay regular quarterly dividends, making them attractive shares to invest in.

In the most recent news, President Donald Trump proposed a 10% cap on credit card interest rates, helping Americans save on the billions of dollars in fees charged on their cards. Since the news broke on January 12, shares of both companies have lost more than 4% each.

Expectations from Visa

The Street expects Visa to report a 14.2% year-over-year jump in adjusted earnings per share (EPS) to $3.14. Sales are forecast to grow 12.3% to $10.68 billion, compared to Q1FY25. Revenue growth is expected to accelerate in FY26, fueled by broad-based increases in payments volume and cross-border transactions. Visa's recent quarters reflect robust performance, with Q4 FY25 revenue hitting $10.7 billion, surpassing estimates.

Notably, Visa recently partnered with BVNK, a top stablecoin infrastructure provider, to launch pilot programs for Visa Direct. This enables businesses to pre-fund payouts using stablecoins such as digital U.S. dollars, allowing instant transfers to recipient wallets and bypassing traditional bank delays, even on weekends and holidays. Businesses no longer need to wait for banks to open on Monday mornings. It streamlines cross-border and real-time payments for Visa's commercial clients, potentially unlocking higher revenues.

Expectations from Mastercard

Mastercard is expected to post an 11.3% year-over-year rise in adjusted EPS to $4.25. Meanwhile, sales are projected to reach $8.78 billion, up 17.2% compared to Q4FY24. Recently, Compass Point analyst Dominick Gabriele upgraded MA stock from a Hold rating to Buy and lifted his price target from $620 to $735 (36.2% upside). He prefers payment networks like Mastercard over other sector players entering 2026, viewing the sector as nearing a "bottom."

Gabriele expects the sector to fully emerge after Q4 earnings reports, paving the way for a stock rebound. He highlighted Mastercard's anticipated superior net revenue growth relative to peers, citing the stock's current valuation multiple as undervalued given these prospects.

Visa or Mastercard: Which Is the Better Card Stock?

According to the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool, analysts have assigned both Visa and Mastercard shares a "Strong Buy" consensus rating. However, MA stock offers a slightly higher upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Intel (INTC) and AMD: Two Chip Stocks to Watch Closely Ahead of Q4 Earnings
data.description:
Intel (INTC) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) are in focus as both chipmakers get ready to report fourth-quarter earnings. Intel shares have bounced back strongly, supported by major deals such as its partnership with Nvidia (NVDA), while AMD heads into earnings with more consistent results and a clearer outlook. Using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool, we look at which of these two stocks Wall Street favors ahead of their Q4 earnings. 

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

With chip stocks under close watch, investors will be looking to earnings and guidance for clues on demand, costs, and how each company is set up for 2026.

Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) 

Intel stock has risen about 116% in 2025, helped by improving investor sentiment, major deals such as its partnership with Nvidia (NVDA), and progress on its 18A chip process. The rally also reflects optimism around Intel's turnaround efforts, including cost cuts and a growing push into foundry services. However, with rivals still ahead in advanced chips, investors will look to upcoming earnings for clearer signs that recent gains can hold.

Looking ahead, Intel is set to report its fourth quarter fiscal 2025 results after the market close on Thursday, January 22. Analysts expect earnings of $0.08 per share, down 38.5% from the year-ago quarter, and revenues of about $13.40 billion, reflecting a 6% year-over-year decrease.

Ahead of the report, Jefferies analyst Blayne Curtis raised his price target on Intel to $45 from $40 but kept a Hold rating. The 5-star analyst said server demand is picking up, but Intel is facing tight production capacity as it shifts output away from low-end PCs toward server products. He also expects the PC market to weaken from March, pressured by higher memory costs and rising prices. On the downside, Curtis warned that margins could come under pressure as Intel ramps up new chips such as Lunar Lake and its 18A process.

Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD)

AMD stock has climbed more than 90% over the past year, driven by strong interest in its AI chips and a series of key partnerships. These include a deal with OpenAI and a new 10-year agreement with Riot Platforms (RIOT), which highlights long-term demand for AMD's hardware. The company is also gaining share in CPUs and continues to push growth with new AI chip launches and upcoming products, as it builds out its position in the AI market.

Looking ahead, AMD will report its Q4 FY25 earnings on Tuesday, February 3, after the market close. Analysts expect earnings of $1.31 per share, up 20% from a year ago, and revenue of about $9.63 billion, reflecting a 26% year-over-year increase.

Ahead of its earnings, top KeyBanc Capital Markets analyst John Vinh upgraded AMD from Sector Weight to Overweight and raised his price target to $270, citing strong demand from data center and cloud customers. He also lifted earnings forecasts to $4.01 per share for 2025 and $7.93 for 2026. While cloud processor deployments were flat month over month and memory shortages continue to cloud some end markets, Vinh sees AMD as a key winner from long-term growth in cloud and AI spending.

INTC or AMD: Which Stock Offers Higher Upside, According to Analysts? 

Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, analysts are more bullish on AMD, which carries a Strong Buy rating and an average price target implying about 22.5% upside. In contrast, Intel holds a Hold rating, with its average price target pointing to around 7.6% downside, reflecting a more cautious view from Wall Street.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: 'Hold Your Horses,' Says Top Investor About Ondas (ONDS) Stock
data.description:
Ondas Holdings (NASDAQ:ONDS) stock has been one of the biggest winners of the current bull market. The defense-focused drone maker's shares have been on a remarkable run over the past year, soaring 504%.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

If you're mulling over whether it's still worth diving in at this point, one top investor who goes by the pseudonym Stone Fox Capital (SFC) thinks it might be best to sit this one out for now.

"Ondas Holdings has rapidly built a leading autonomous drone defense business, but its valuation now appears disconnected from near-term financial targets," says SFC, who's ranked among the top 2% of stock experts on TipRanks.

It's true that the counter-UAS (counter-unmanned aircraft systems) market is positioned for significant growth in future orders, while the conflict in Ukraine has heightened demand for counter-drone defense capabilities. Against this backdrop, Ondas provides a "leading solution in this growing sector."

Sentrycs CEO Tal Cohen recently noted that global demand for counter-drone technologies is expected to expand from approximately $2.4 billion in 2024 to more than $10.5 billion by 2030. In practice, SFC thinks it's not unreasonable to imagine the market could be significantly larger than $10 billion, as countries across the Western world will require systems to secure borders and protect airspace from drone threats.

But Ondas' backlog is still rather modest. The company exited Q3 with a backlog of just $22 million. Management outlined an initial 2026 revenue target of $110 million, and while this represents roughly a 200% increase from the 2025 target, the absolute revenue level remains, according to the investor, "relatively small."

Meanwhile, recent equity financings have generated $855 million in capital, with an additional $1.5 billion potentially available through warrant exercises if the share price reaches $20, which could lift the company's market value to nearly $10 billion. The 5-star investor thinks the market does not appear to have "fully grasped the increased valuation" resulting from these actions.

Additionally, a big risk revolves around the fact Ondas lacks an established, long-term leadership position in the drone market. There is no assurance that the company can sustain a technology advantage, and the current valuation already reflects expectations for several years of substantial growth.

The key takeaway for investors, says SFC, is that while Ondas presents a "very promising growth story," it has already seen its share price rally to the point where it's hard to justify wading in at current levels.

Accordingly, SFC rates Ondas stock as Neutral. (To watch Stone Fox Capital's track record, click here)

Turning to Wall Street, this stock offers something of a conundrum right now. On the one hand, ONDS boasts a Strong Buy rating, based on a unanimous 8 Buys. However, the average target of $12 sits slightly below the current share price. With this in mind, watch out for either price target hikes or rating downgrades shortly. (See ONDS stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Cybercab Sighting Seems to Confirm a Feature Tesla Owners Have Been Requesting for Years
data.description:
A recent sighting of EV maker Tesla's (TSLA) Cybercab prototype in Chicago appears to confirm a practical feature that owners and observers have been requesting for years: a rear camera washer. Indeed, photos that have been circulating on social media show the autonomous two-seater covered in dust and road grime. However, the rear camera area looks noticeably cleaner than the rest of the vehicle. In addition, watermarks on the trunk further suggest that the Cybercab is equipped with a rear camera washer.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This detail matters because external cameras are critical to autonomous driving, especially in harsh weather. Interestingly, Tesla owners have been requesting camera washers for a long time, particularly in snowy or wet regions where dirt and slush can quickly limit visibility and reduce system performance. While only the rear camera washer was clearly visible, the sighting suggests that Tesla may add similar cleaning systems to other exterior cameras.

This would be a logical step for the company, especially since the Cybercab will not have a steering wheel, pedals, or LiDAR and is designed to continuously carry passengers. More importantly, much of Tesla's current share price is tied to the Cybercab's potential. Therefore, it is important that the system functions as safely as possible.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $397.47 per share implies 9.2% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Trump's New Tariffs Could Send the UK Spiraling into a Recession
data.description:
President Trump will impose an additional 10% tariff on eight countries, including the United Kingdom, starting on February 1. Afterward, the rate will rise to 25% on June 1. The tariffs will remain in place until the U.S. secures a deal to acquire Greenland.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That could send the UK into a recession, according to Capital Economics. The firm estimates that a 25% tariff on top of the country's existing 10% tariff will decrease its gross domestic product (GDP) by 0.75%, equivalent to a $25.15 billion hit. "With the UK economy currently growing by 0.2-0.3pc a quarter, if this hit came all at once it could trigger a recession," said Capital Economics Chief UK Economist Paul Dales.

Starmer Calls for 'Calm Discussion' on Greenland Dispute

Unlike several other European leaders, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has talked down the prospect of retaliatory tariffs and instead proposed a negotiation process. "I think that this can be resolved, and should be resolved, through calm discussion," he said. Starmer added that imposing tariffs is the wrong approach when addressing differences within an alliance and that the people of Greenland should have control over their future.

European Union leaders are scheduled to meet this Thursday for an emergency summit to discuss their options, which include reviving a $103 billion tariff package on U.S. goods that was shelved last year after the two sides reached a trade agreement.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'Get Ready for the Next Upswing,' Says Analyst About Ethereum (ETH)
data.description:
2025 ended in disappointing fashion for Bitcoin (BTC-USD) investors. While many prognosticators expected its price to peak above $150,000, Bitcoin's rally stalled and the cryptocurrency actually saw out the year 6% into the red. Of course, as is always the case, Bitcoin dragged down with it all other tokens.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, looking specifically at the case of the second biggest crypto Ethereum (ETH-USD), Standard Chartered's Head of Digital Assets Research, Geoff Kendrick, thinks its prospects have gotten better, and as such, he eventually sees the ETH-BTC price making its way back to its 2021 highs of around 0.08.

Kendrick believes there are several factors that should support outperformance from Ethereum. For one, although ETF and digital asset treasury (DAT) inflows have slowed across the sector, ongoing accumulation by Bitmine Immersion Technologies, the largest ETH-focused DAT, provides a relative tailwind for Ethereum.

The DAT accumulation of digital assets has largely stalled following the late-2025 collapse in mNAVs (market net asset value), a valuation metric that compares a company's market capitalization with the value of the digital assets it holds. Elevated mNAVs above 1.0 had previously enabled DATs to effectively convert $1.00 into $1.50, but several major DATs now trade below 1.0, including Strategy and SharpLink Gaming. These valuations saw little improvement even after MSCI confirmed on January 6 that DATs would not be excluded from its indices, following the earlier October 2025 review announcement.

In contrast, Bitmine has maintained an mNAV of roughly 1.1–1.2, allowing it to continue accumulating ETH at a "reasonably fast pace." Bitmine now holds 3.4% of ETH in circulation and remains on track to reach its stated 5% target by late Q1 or early Q2. "Against this backdrop," Kendrick says, "DAT buying of ETH appears more sustainable than DAT buying of BTC.

Second, Kendrick is still "very bullish" on the outlook for stablecoins, RWAs (real-world assets) and DeFi (decentralised finance), segments where Ethereum continues to hold a dominant position. By the end of 2028, Kendrick expects both stablecoins and RWAs to reach market caps of $2 trillion, rising from current levels of $308 billion for stablecoins (according to Artemis), and $40 billion for RWAs (according to The Block).

Lastly, In June 2025, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin outlined plans to scale ETH's layer 1 throughput by 10x over the following two to three years, a plan that so far "appears to be working," with Kendrick's analysis showing that "higher throughput translates into higher market cap."

Despite Kendrick's more constructive view on ETH relative to BTC, the analyst has reduced his ETH-USD forecasts for 2026–28 to account for BTC's weakness. That said, he also increased his ETH-USD forecast for end-2029 and expects the price to reach $40,000 by the end of 2030.
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: 2026's Big Air Race: Does Boeing (NYSE:BA) Come Out Ahead of Airbus?
data.description:
One thing we can say about 2025 in aerospace was that it was extremely competitive. Aerospace stock Boeing (BA) managed to do quite well with larger aircraft, while its immediate rival, Airbus (EADSY) managed to come out on top with smaller commercial aircraft. But what about 2026? We are barely three weeks into the new year, and it is already shaping up to be fundamentally different from 2025. Who is likely to come out on top this year? The answer might surprise you.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Boeing: A Come-From-Behind…Win?

There is a lot to like about Boeing's 2026. The clear point in Boeing's favor here is the labor contracts. 2024 saw the union in Washington go on strike. 2025, meanwhile, saw IAM District 837 in St. Louis go on strike from late August to November. With both strikes resolved–and a third with the SPEEA also resolved to the tentative agreement level just days ago–this means Boeing's labor troubles are likely to be quiet for 2026.

It also does not hurt that the 737 MAX 10 is closer than ever to certification. Getting that plane into the field this year would be a huge game-changer, assuming it can get through the certification process. Granted, that is a long assumption; the 737 MAX 10's certification process has been delayed before, and could easily be delayed again. It remains, however, that getting it in play in 2026 would turn things around quite well.

However, Boeing will see a lot of downsides to 2026. The first is that the boost of President Trump's trade deal tour that Boeing got in 2025 will not be happening again this year. While this means fewer orders, the good news is that the process of selling a plane means that Boeing will make money for filling orders, which it has plenty of on hand.

Boeing also has a production cap from the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to consider. The cap has been raised once, and there are good signs that it will be raised at least once more before the end of 2026. Given that Boeing has cooperated with the FAA in every particular since the last time the cap was raised, and no significant issues have arisen in the meantime, the idea that the FAA would again raise the cap is logically valid. It is possible that the cap may not be raised, and either way, the cap is still there and will be there for the foreseeable future.

Airbus labors under no such formal, regulatory cap.

Airbus: Building on Its Successes

Airbus' year looks pretty bright by comparison. While it lacks some of Boeing's advantages, it also lacks several of Boeing's drawbacks.

Airbus is already running at capacity right now. The reports suggest that Airbus is pretty much maxed out in terms of production right now, so it is producing at a rate that is likely to surpass Boeing. However, that also suggests that any problem at Airbus—any supply chain hiccup, any mechanical breakdown, or the like—is likely to hit Airbus and knock it off this perch. The same goes for any issues with its engine supplier, which will hit Airbus unusually hard.

Airbus has an advantage in public perception as well. Boeing is perhaps best known in the field, but Boeing is also connected to being the aircraft maker that recently had a lot of mechanical failures. Airbus can comfortably work from that position of strength, but again, if anything goes wrong for Airbus, it loses that advantage. If something happens to Boeing, Boeing supporters would note that it can bounce back faster, simply by referring to everything it has already done to fix things from the last failure.

Airbus also, at last report, has a larger backlog to work through than Boeing does. This means that Airbus can put production capacity that is superior to Boeing's right now to work harder, for longer. This should ultimately let it pull out ahead.

Basically, Airbus is on top of the game right now, but Boeing can better absorb any kind of failure or downturn. Airbus, should it end up in a similar position, will be hit as hard as Boeing was the first time these issues came to light.

So Who Wins?

Granted, your definition of "win" may be different from anyone else's, including my own. But it looks like this race will be Airbus' to lose this year. Airbus has a greater backlog and no limits on production capacity save its own. Boeing is still fighting to win back trust and capability from before the series of malfunctions hit. So while Boeing will put on a good showing this year, Airbus is likely to come out on top.

Which Stock Comes Out on Top?

When comparing the two stocks, Boeing is rated a Strong Buy with a 4.04% upside potential on a $257.69 average price target. Meanwhile, Airbus offers a 170.01% upside potential against an average price target of $170.46 per share, rated a Moderate Buy.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: OpenAI Says 2026 Will Be a Turning Point for Practical AI Adoption
data.description:
Microsoft-backed (MSFT) AI firm OpenAI wants 2026 to be the year that its artificial intelligence tools move from impressive demos to everyday use, according to a new blog post from CFO Sarah Friar. More specifically, she said that the main goal is to close the gap between what AI is capable of and how people, companies, and governments actually use it day-to-day. Friar pointed to areas like healthcare, scientific research, and enterprise software as places where better AI can quickly lead to better results.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

She also explained that OpenAI's revenue growth is closely tied to how much computing power it can access. In fact, Friar pointed out that OpenAI's compute capacity increased from about 0.2 gigawatts in 2023 to roughly 1.9 gigawatts in 2025, while its annual revenue run rate rose from $2 billion to more than $20 billion over the same period. In her view, this shows that more infrastructure directly leads to faster adoption and higher revenue.

As a result, OpenAI has entered major infrastructure partnerships, including a widely discussed agreement with chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) that was announced last September. Indeed, Nvidia said that it would support OpenAI with up to $100 billion in AI systems with at least 10 gigawatts of capacity, which is an amount comparable to the power used by millions of U.S. homes. However, Nvidia later warned investors that there is no guarantee the deal will move forward. Nevertheless, Friar noted that OpenAI now works with multiple compute providers, thereby allowing it to plan and scale more reliably.

Is MSFT Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 32 Buys and two Holds assigned in the last three months. Furthermore, the average MSFT price target of $631.36 per share implies 37.3% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
